other_observers: Steve Mlodinow, Mark Peterson; Steve Mlodinow found and identified it
specific_location: at and near feeders on back side of Moose Visitor Center
description: A large sparrow, with dark brown lateral crown stripes and eyeline; buffy-tan central crown stripe and superciliary; dull yellow patch in supraloral area; brownish-gray auriculars; white chin; thin, black lateral throat stripes; white malar stripes; back streaked with dark brown and buffy-tan; chest grayish with vague darker streaking; sides/flanks with medium brown streaks; wings rufous-brown with white tips to median and greater coverts forming wing bars; tail long and roundedBehaviors: foraging under willows and then under feedersCall: none heardPlumage: Formative
